title: THE PHYSICS LAB HACK
subtitle: Origin of the Name
description: While debugging orbital rotation formulas in senior physics, we noticed that computing alignments along the 'Principal Axis' is what prevents systems from wobbling out of control. We built our first local peer-to-peer data router during that very lab period, naming our group after that stabilizing stabilizer coordinate.

title: COMPRESSED CHIP CLUSTERS
subtitle: First Viral Output
description: Faced with extremely tight browser sandboxes, we designed a differential JSON parser that compressed structured compiler records by 88% natively in the thread stack. A simple showcase post on HackerNews went viral overnight, leading to 1,500 developer testers in under 48 hours.

title: THE ZERO-CONFIG MANIFESTO
subtitle: Ditching the Boilerplate
description: We realized developers are spending more than 20% of their creative energy configuring configurations instead of writing code. We decided to build simple, visual, spatial modules that run client-side to strip away setup overhead entirely. This led to our 'Zero-Config' focus that underpins all our projects.

title: UNLEASHING GLOBAL NODES
subtitle: Teenagers Defying Limits
description: Operating as a fully remote teen-led network across coastlines, we are shipping low-layer developer speedups, spatial shells, and WebGPU draft canvases. We prove that premium, professional, hardware-optimized production systems do not require complex corporate structures—just obsessive craft.
